data.title: NUO Unit Finalises Acquisition in Bialetti, Launches Tender Offer Of 0.467 EUR/Share Aimed At Delisting
data.description:
Bialetti Industrie SpA on behalf of Nuo Octagon SpA, Bialetti Investimenti SpA, Bialetti Holding Srl:

  • SAID ON THURSDAY NUO'S UNIT OCTAGON BIDCO HAS ACQUIRED 78.567% STAKE IN BIALETTI

  • OCTAGON BIDCO HAS LAUNCHED TENDER OFFER AIMED AT DELISTING REMAINING BIALETTI SHARES AT A PRICE OF 0.467 EUR/SHARE

Further company coverage:

data.title: Mr Price Group Posts FY HEPS Up 10.7%
data.description:
Mr Price Group Ltd :

  • JSE: MRP - ANNUAL RESULTS FOR THE 52 WEEKS ENDED 29 MARCH 2025 AND CASH DIVIDEND DECLARATION

  • MR PRICE FY REVENUE ZAR 40,900 MILLION VS. IBES ESTIMATE ZAR 40,049 MILLION

data.title: Palm rangebound but set for fourth weekly gain
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures traded in a tight range on Friday, as rising production and inventories continued to pressure the market but the contract was still set for a fourth straight weekly gain as firmer rival oils offered some support.

The benchmark palm oil contract for August delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange shed 2 ringgit, or 0.05%, to 3,901 ringgit ($922.88) a metric ton at the midday break.

The contract has risen 1.11% so far this week.

Crude palm oil futures traded marginally lower, reversing earlier gains as concerns over rising output and stock levels in the country continued to weigh on sentiment, said David Ng, a proprietary trader at Kuala Lumpur-based trading firm Iceberg X Sdn Bhd.

"We see support at 3,800 ringgit and resistance at 4,000 ringgit."

Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) rose 0.73%, while its palm oil contract fell 0.22%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) gained 1.24%.

Palm oil tracks the price movements of rival edible oils as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

Oil prices slipped but were on track for their first weekly gain in three weeks after U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping resumed trade talks, raising hopes for growth and stronger demand in the world's two largest economies. O/R

Weaker crude oil futures make palm a less attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.

The ringgit , palm's currency of trade, remained unchanged against the U.S. dollar.

($1 = 4.2270 ringgit)

data.title: Airbus Target Cut to EUR183 From EUR209 by Citi
Airbus Cut to Neutral From Buy by Citi
India's forex reserves fall to $691.5 billion as on May 30
data.description:
India's foreign exchange reserves (INFXR=ECI) stood at $691.5 billion as of May 30, the governor of the country's central bank said on Friday, down $1.2 billion from the previous week and coming off near-eight month highs.

The country's FX reserves are now about $13.4 billion below their all-time high hit in September 2024.

"These (reserves) are sufficient to fund more than 11 months of goods imports and about 96% of external debt outstanding," Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra said in a statement while announcing the monetary policy decision.

Changes in foreign currency assets, expressed in dollar terms, include the effect of appreciation or depreciation of other currencies held in the reserves.

For the week ended May 30, the rupee notched a weekly drop amid uncertainty around U.S. tariffs. The currency had risen to a six-month high in May but shed its gains through the month.

The rupee was little changed at 85.8050 per U.S. dollar on Friday, after the RBI cut its key repo rate by a larger-than-expected 50 basis points, a third consecutive reduction, and slashed the reserve ratio for banks.

Foreign exchange reserves include India's reserve tranche position in the International Monetary Fund.

data.title: CAB Cakaran Unit Hit By Cyberattack
data.description:
CAB Cakaran's unit, Farm's Best Food Industries, reported a cyberattack on its accounting system on Wednesday, according to a Thursday Malaysian bourse filing.

Farm's Best Food Industries quickly activated security protocols and began assessing the damage and recovering data.

Internal IT teams and external cybersecurity experts are working to restore full system functionality, the filing said.

The company is still evaluating the extent of the breach and any financial impact, it added.

data.title: South Africa's Mr Price posts 10.1% rise in annual profit
data.description:
South African fashion retailer Mr Price reported a 10.1% increase in full-year earnings on Friday on strong second-half performance as it gained market share.

Diluted headline earnings per share, a key profit measure in South Africa, rose to 13.79 rand in the 52 weeks ended March 29.

The retailer declared a final dividend of 593.50 cents.

data.title: Muyuan Foods' Pig Sales Revenue Jumps 27% in May
data.description:
Muyuan Foods' revenue from selling pigs in May jumped 27% year on year to 12.3 billion yuan, according to a Shenzhen bourse filing on Friday.

The Chinese pork producer sold 6.4 million pigs during the month, 30% higher than a year earlier.

The average sales price of commercial hogs was 14.52 yuan per kilogram, down 6.4% from the previous year, the filing said.

Muyuan's shares rose over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Hanmi Semiconductor Forms Team to Support HBM4 Equipment Rollout
data.description:
Hanmi Semiconductor set up a dedicated team called Silver Phoenix to support its TC Bonder 4 equipment for HBM4 production, the chipmaker said in a press release on Thursday.

The team, comprising around 50 semiconductor equipment specialists, will focus on optimizing TC Bonder 4 units installed at customer sites manufacturing high-bandwidth memory (HBM) for AI semiconductors, the release said.

The team also has 30 hybrid four-wheel drive SUVs to ensure swift technical response.

data.title: HSBC chair's AIA move less startling than it looks
data.description:
Mark Tucker will lead the board of the $90 bln Hong Kong insurer he once ran. That may seem like a backward step. But it's an appealing role for someone with so much China experience. And his willingness to shake things up, as he demonstrated at the UK bank, could benefit AIA.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Antony Currie on Bluesky and Linkedin.

CONTEXT NEWS

Mark Tucker is to become chair of AIA on October 1, the Hong Kong-listed insurance group said on June 6. Tucker is to retire as chair of HSBC on September 30, the UK bank said on June 6, a little over a month after revealing that he intended to retired by the end of this year.

Tucker was chief executive officer of AIA from 2010 to 2017.

data.title: India Central Bank Delivers Larger-Than-Expected Rate Cut to Support Growth
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao

India's central bank delivered a larger-than-expected rate cut, as sound economic conditions at home and tariff risks abroad make a case for more policy easing.

The Reserve Bank of India's monetary policy committee on Friday voted to cut the policy repo rate to 5.50% from 6.00%, a steep cut of 50 basis points.

All 13 economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal had expected the central bank to deliver a cut, continuing the easing cycle the central bank began in February.

"The global backdrop remains fragile and highly fluid," RBI Gov. Sanjay Malhotra said. "As growth inflation trade-offs is becoming more challenging, monetary authorities are starting out a more cautious and carefully calibrated trajectory," he said.

In a keenly watched change, the RBI also voted to change its monetary policy stance to neutral from accommodative.

The RBI's decision comes as cooling inflation and solid economic growth in India give policymakers space to continue their dovish pivot.

However, focus remains on the threat posed by President Trump's sweeping tariffs on trading partners, which have been put in legal limbo by a U.S. trade court ruling.

India, which faces a 26% tariff, has been in talks with the U.S. The talks are expected to result in a deal soon.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: Nomura Tips Singapore to Avoid Recession, Lowers Inflation Forecasts
data.description:
Nomura Holdings lowered Singapore's headline and core inflation forecasts to 0.7% from 0.9%, according to Nomura's Asia Economic Monthly report published Friday.

However, the Japanese research firm's analysts maintained 2025 GDP growth forecast for the city-state at 2.0%, stating that the "technical recession" is likely to be avoided thanks to "surging export growth."

Industrial output is expected to significantly slowdown in H2 due to payback effects. However, Nomura expects the Singaporean government to roll out large fiscal support measures, which will provide a "significant cushion," the report said.

On the jobs front, the analysts see a gradual rise in the unemployment rate to 2.4% at end-2025 from 2.1% in Q1.

Nomura further expects the Monetary Authority of Singapore to ease its policy further in July via a reduction in the slope of the policy band.

Additionally, the analysts forecast an overall fiscal deficit of 0.1% of GDP in fiscal 2025 ending March 31, 2026.

data.title: India's Nifty Financial Services index climbs on larger-than-expected rate cut
data.description:
** India's Nifty Financial Services Index jump 1.2%

** India central bank cuts key lending rate by a larger-than-expected 50 bps in a bid to support economic growth

** Financial services index was down 0.2% ahead of the policy decision

** 19 of the 20 stocks in the index trade higher

** .NIFTYFIN is up 13% so far in 2025 vs a 5 rise in Nifty 50

data.title: Japan says emergency rice imports, other options considered to stabilise prices
data.description:
Japan said on Friday that "all options" were being considered to stabilise rice prices, including the use of up to 100,000 metric tons of tariff-free staple rice the government imports a year, and further emergency imports beyond that quota.

WHY IT'S IMPORTANT

The doubling of rice prices since last year has become a major concern for consumers as well as policymakers ahead of key elections in Tokyo and nationwide in June and July.

The government has so far earmarked about 600,000 metric tons of rice for release from its emergency stockpile in an effort to lower prices. Koizumi has said he was ready to release the entire stock - or another 300,000 tons - as needed, but it remains to be seen how much that would help prices fall.

KEY QUOTES

"If you look at other products, (emergency imports) are something that we naturally turn to. When bird flu causes a shortage of eggs, for example, we've imported from Brazil," Japan's Agriculture Minister Shinjiro Koizumi told a press conference.

"Maybe rice has always been seen as sacred ground but we have to drop that thinking and consider all options to achieve stable prices."

BY THE NUMBERS

Japan has a limit of 100,000 metric tons a year of tariff-free "minimum access" staple rice imports, as agreed with the World Trade Organisation. As homegrown rice prices have soared, demand for cheaper, foreign-made rice has surged, even for imports outside this tariff-free quota.

Rice prices in Japanese supermarkets averaged 4,260 yen ($29.62) per 5 kg (11 pounds) in the week to May 25, about double the level from a year earlier.

CONTEXT

Shortly after taking over as agriculture minister last month, Koizumi ended the slow and cumbersome distribution method of stockpiled rice to cooperatives and wholesalers via auction. The emergency rice is now being sold directly to retailers, which are generally selling it for about 2,000 yen per 5 kg.

WHAT'S NEXT

How much average rice prices come down across the market and what further policies, if any, are taken will remain in focus as Japan approaches crucial elections.

($1 = 143.8100 yen)

data.title: Estonia Inflation Rate Steady in May
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Estonia stood at 4.5% in May 2025, unchanged from the previous month.

Prices rose mainly for transport (6.2% vs 5% in April), driven by the car tax introduced at the start of the year and higher passenger transport costs.

Additionally, costs continued to increase for communication (1.6% vs 0.4%), recreation and entertainment (4.9% vs 4.6%), and hotels, cafes, and restaurants (4.9% vs 4.4%).

On the other hand, inflation moderated for food and non-alcoholic beverages (7.1% vs 7.4%), housing (2.1% vs 3.1%), and health (9.9% vs 11%), while it remained unchanged for clothing and footwear (at -4.5%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices rose by 0.1% in May, easing from a 1% advance in the preceding period.

data.description:

The number of people who applied for unemployment benefits last week jumped to an eight-month, but the details of the government's report show little evidence of a tariff-related rise in layoffs.

data.title: Dassault Systemes Aims to Double Earnings by 2029
data.description:
(05:00 GMT) *Dassault Systemes Aims to Double Non-IFRS Diluted EPS by 2029
(05:02 GMT) *Dassault Systemes: New Target Reflects Gradual Acceleration in Top-Line Growth
(05:03 GMT) *Dassault Systemes: This Allows Adoption of 3D UNIV+RSES Platform to Deliver Full Potential
(05:04 GMT) *Dassault Systemes: Target Also Reflects Continued Strategic Capital Allocation
(05:04 GMT) *Dassault Systemes: Capital Allocation Includes Targeted M&A
(05:15 GMT) Dassault Systemes Aims to Double Earnings by 2029

By Adria Calatayud

Dassault Systemes said it aims to double earnings excluding exceptional items by 2029 as it looks to deploy its generative artificial intelligence offering.

The French software maker said Friday that its updated midterm financial ambition calls for doubling its non-IFRS diluted earnings per share by 2029. For 2024, the company's non-IFRS diluted earnings per share stood at 0.90 euros ($1.03).

The company said its target reflects a gradual acceleration in top-line growth, the scale-up of 3D UNIV+RSES--an offering that embeds multiple generative AI technologies--, and its capital allocation, including targeted mergers and acquisitions.

Dassault Systemes, like other European software companies, presents its figures as two sets of numbers. One set is based on the International Financial Reporting Standards--an international accounting method that seeks to provide a global reporting standard--though analysts and investors tend to follow non-IFRS numbers. Non-IFRS figures exclude share-based compensation, restructuring expenses and acquisition-related charges.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: Helios Energy to Prioritize Bringing Texas Oil, Gas Project Wells into Production
data.description:
Helios Energy's priorities included bringing its discovered wells at the Presidio oil and gas project in Texas into production and establishing operational cash flow, its Managing Director, Philipp Kin, said in a shareholder update on Friday.

Concurrently with establishing cash flow from production, the firm will allocate capital to targeted drilling and expansion efforts to unlock the wider potential of the Ojinaga and Eagle Ford formations at the project.

It is also evaluating new well targets based on W.D. Von Gonten Engineering's reservoir modelling and existing seismic data.

A reduction in staff and administration, and corporate costs has also been a priority for the firm, and it said it had reached a "level appropriate for a junior explorer."

It continues to work with W.D. Von Gonten Engineering in order to advance production planning.

data.title: Japan Coincident Index Lowest in 5 Months
data.description:
Japan's index of coincident economic indicators—which tracks factory output, employment, and retail sales—fell to 115.5 in April 2025 from a downwardly revised 115.8, preliminary estimates showed.

This marked the lowest level since last November, as persistently high costs continued to weigh on private consumption and the burden of steep US tariffs intensified.

Japan’s trade performance was mixed during the month: imports declined, while exports grew at the slowest pace in seven months, dampened by the impact of new US tariffs.

data.title: Xplora Technologies Secures First Orders for HMD Fusion X1 Smartphone
data.description:
Norwegian children's smartwatch company Xplora Technologies received its first customer orders for the HMD Fusion X1 smartphone, developed in partnership with HMD.

The new device is designed to address growing concerns around teenagers' digital habits, the need for parental control, online safety, and more responsible smartphone usage, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Catapult Price Target Raised 27% to A$7.10/Share by Jefferies
Whitehaven Coal Finalises Enterprise Agreement at Narrabri, Drops 7/7 Roster Push, Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Whitehaven Coal's negotiations with Narrabri Underground coal miners concluded with the miners getting their preferred midweek/weekend roster, against the company's proposed 7/7 roster, according to the Mining and Energy Union statement published Friday.

The new enterprise agreement includes annual pay increases of 5%, 4%, and 4%, improved shift loadings of up to 20%, and increased retention and attendance bonuses.

The maximum retention bonus has been increased to AU$15,000 from AU$10,000, at AU$1,000 for every year of service, and an attendance bonus has been secured by the workers of up to AU$5,000.

The new agreement will backpay the workers to December 2024, covering 21 weeks.

The agreement also secured a three-year term, despite the company's preference for four, and maintained the maximum 10.5-hour shift length.

Under a new clause added to the agreement, the company cannot implement such a roster in the future.

Whitehaven Coal's shares rose 3% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Eastern Polymer Group Likely Shifted Focus to Cost Control From Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Eastern Polymer Group has likely shifted its focus to cost control from growth, CGS International's Pornthipa Rayabsangduan says in a report. The polymer and plastic converter company turns defensive and targets flat revenue growth in FY 2026, the analyst says. For FY 2026, the Thai company is aiming to turn its Australian operations around through cost control, process efficiency and operational realignment. The brokerage cuts its FY 2026-2027 core EPS estimates for the company by 8.9%-12.2% to reflect changes in some of its assumptions. It lowers the stock's target price to THB2.50 from THB3.62 with an unchanged hold rating. Shares are unchanged at THB2.44. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Ningbo Zhoushan Port Expects Container, Cargo Throughput to Rise in May
data.description:
Ningbo Zhoushan Port's container throughput in May is expected to have increased 7.1% year on year to 4.5 million twenty-foot equivalent units or TEUs, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

The Chinese port operator's cargo throughput is anticipated to have risen 7.7% to 107.6 million tons from a year earlier, the filing said.

data.title: Trump vs Musk: Crypto Market Turns Red, Tesla Closes 14% Lower
data.description:
A public fallout between Donald Trump and Elon Musk has rattled financial markets, sending Tesla stock tumbling and dragging the crypto sector into a broad risk-off retreat.

The rift came to a head on June 5 during a White House meeting with the German chancellor, where , who until recently had been one of his closest advisers. “I’m very disappointed in Elon,” Trump said. “I’ve helped Elon a lot.”

Musk wasted no time responding. In a pointed post on X, he accused Trump of ingratitude and fired back, “Without me, Trump would have lost the election.” The exchange marked a dramatic unraveling of their once-powerful alliance, built during Trump’s second term.Musk Swipe at Trump Adds Fuel to Market Volatility

Tensions escalated further when . The remark drew immediate attention, although any such effort is considered politically impossible, as Republicans currently hold majorities in both chambers of Congress.

Yes — Elon Musk (@elonmusk)

Markets quickly reflected the fallout. Bitcoin dropped as low as $1100,500 on Thursday before recovering above $102,700. Over $324 million have been liquidated in Bitcoin longs in the last 24 hours, according to . The broader crypto market fell nearly 5% during the same time.

The $TRUMP meme coin, which had surged during the peak of their alliance, slid 10%. Analysts say Musk’s influence in the crypto space, combined with the political uncertainty, has introduced fresh instability into an already volatile market.Tesla Plunges as Rift With Trump Threatens Federal Support

Tesla shares, meanwhile, suffered a sharper decline. The stock closed 14% lower on Thursday and has fallen 16% since Musk began attacking Trump’s domestic policy bill last week.

The market was hit hard by the Trump and Musk issues.But in fact, the impact of these issue is unlikely to have a significant impact on the crypto market. Just, whales used this issue to liquidate their long positions.NASDAQ fell due to Tesla, and this impact may not be…— CW (@CW8900)

It now trades roughly 33% below where it stood on Inauguration Day, showing growing fears that the feud could jeopardize billions in federal subsidies and contracts vital to Tesla’s long-term prospects.

Until recently, Musk had served as a key informal adviser to Trump, helping shape major policy decisions and frequently representing the administration abroad.

His sudden departure from that inner circle has left a noticeable void in tech-policy alignment, with markets now unsure of how the administration will proceed on key issues such as electric vehicles and space infrastructure.

Adding to the uncertainty, Musk has hinted at launching a new political movement altogether. That possibility, along with the risk of a regulatory reversal or funding pullback, has left investors bracing for a more hostile environment. And that’s not just for Tesla, but for the broader tech and crypto sectors that have often moved in tandem with Musk’s influence.

The clash has become more than a political sideshow. It is a rare case of personality-driven politics colliding with market fundamentals. For now, both crypto and Tesla appear firmly caught in the crossfire.

data.title: France's Dassault Systemes pushes EPS target timeline to 2029
data.description:
French software company Dassault Systemes extended the target period of its medium-term earnings per share (EPS) forecast by one year on Friday.

The group, which sells its software to automakers, plane makers and industrial companies, said ahead of its investor day event that it aims to reach non-IFRS EPS of between 2.20 euros and 2.40 euros by 2029, compared with the previous 2028 target.

data.title: Kuaishou Accelerates AI in Content and Commercial Ecosystem — Market Talk
data.description: Kuaishou Technology has accelerated its application of AI in its content and commercial ecosystem, Jefferies analysts write in a note. The company's AI model could help deepen its understanding of users' interests and enables precise matching backed by large models, they add. Its AI model, Kling, has a large addressable market with accelerated commercialization, they say. Kling stood out with its video and image quality compared with its overseas peers, Jefferies says. The brokerage maintains its buy rating for the stock at a target price of HK$81.00. Shares last at HK$58.10. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)
